{"step_by_step_thinking": "In prokaryotes, the Shine-Dalgarno sequence is recognized by the 30S subunit of the ribosome, which helps in the initiation of translation by aligning the ribosome with the start codon. tRNA is involved in carrying amino acids to the ribosome during translation, but it does not directly bind the mRNA to the ribosome. 7 methyl guanosine capping and poly A tail are involved in mRNA processing and stability, not in binding the mRNA to the ribosome.", "answer_choice": "B"}
